one_line_thesis: Dominant e-commerce + AWS platform compounding at scale (FY2025 $717B sales, $78B NI) with AWS re-accelerating (+28% Q1-26) and high-margin ads/chips engines emerging — but AI capex has temporarily collapsed FCF, requiring disciplined valuation and capex ROI monitoring.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Amazon runs four reinforcing engines: retail logistics density, AWS, high-margin advertising (more than $70 billion trailing twelve months), and custom silicon (Trainium, Graviton, Nitro). Q1 2026 consolidated operating income $23.9 billion (+30%) and AWS re-acceleration (+28%) confirm operating momentum. [Fact] Trailing twelve-month operating cash flow is $148.5 billion, but trailing free cash flow is only $1.2 billion because capital spending consumed nearly all operating cash flow. [Fact] Q1 2026 net income $30.3 billion includes roughly $16.8 billion pre-tax gain on Anthropic and must b...

decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

gaps.question: Map every material operating, asset, liability, financing, and option claim owned by one diluted share with non-overlapping overlap keys.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary filings plus an independently reproducible component ownership map.
gaps.acceptance_test: Every material claim is identified once, with provisional low/base/high if incomplete, and no double counting.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: component schedule scaffolded with unique overlap keys; ownership map pending primary bridge.

gaps.question: Reconcile debt, cash, reserves, dilution, and material options including probability, timing, capital, failure value, and overlap control.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary filings or contracts plus an independently reproducible calculation and relevant cross-check.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high bridge, resolves overlap, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: liabilities, reserves, and options are scheduled; primary downside and capital bridge pending.

gaps.question: Reconcile the primary owner-cash or NAV bridge to primary filings with causal low/base/high cases.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary filings or contracts plus an independently reproducible calculation and relevant cross-check.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high bridge, resolves overlap, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: partially_met: component schedule scaffolded; primary owner-cash or NAV bridge pending.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AMZN, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Derived from inputs.shares_millions in the pre-existing valuation.json; [HUMAN REVIEW] confirm against the latest primary filing.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 10-Q: net sales $181.5B (+17%); AWS +28%; TTM OCF $148.5B vs capex $147.3B
FY2025 10-K: net sales $716.9B (+12%); three reportable segments; AI capex cycle disclosed
Management frames multi-year AI infrastructure build; trailing FCF near zero while OCF remains >$140B
Continued hyperscaler-scale capital spending on data centers and custom silicon (Trainium/Graviton)
Additional Anthropic investment; Q1 2026 marks create GAAP net income noise for owners
Andy Jassy CEO (since 2021) executing AWS-led growth and retail efficiency phase post-pandemic
FTC and state antitrust scrutiny of marketplace and cloud practices (ongoing risk)
Jeff Bezos transitions to Executive Chair; Jassy becomes CEO
Whole Foods acquisition expands grocery footprint and Prime ecosystem density
AWS launched as separate infrastructure business; now majority of operating income
Prime membership program deepens retail switching costs and shipping economics
IPO; begins public-market compounding arc (three stock splits through 2022)

industry.structure: Global e-commerce is fragmented but scale leaders (Amazon, Walmart, Alibaba) dominate logistics-heavy markets. Cloud infrastructure is an oligopoly among AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud with high switching costs and massive capital barriers.
industry.share_shift: AWS gained AI workload share in 2025–2026 as enterprise inference demand accelerated; retail share stable in North America with same-day delivery density as differentiator versus pure-play e-commerce.
industry.trend: Hyperscalers are in a capital-intensive AI build cycle: near-term free cash flow suppressed while operating cash flow and segment margins remain strong; advertising and custom silicon raise mix and margin over time.

top_claims.claim: Amazon runs four reinforcing engines: retail logistics density, AWS, high-margin advertising (>$70B TTM), and custom silicon (Trainium/Graviton/Nitro). Q1 2026 consolidated operating income $23.9B (+30%) and AWS re-acceleration (+28%) confirm operating momentum. [Fact] TTM operating cash flow is $148.5B, but TTM FCF is
We expect **3.2%** per year over 7 years at **~$263.36** on **$5.35/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Prolonged AI capex overshoot (TTM purchases $147.3B vs OCF $148.5B) without AWS, ads, and silicon returns showing up in normalized free cash flow per share.
AWS growth deceleration and margin compression from hyperscaler price competition.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Prolonged AI capex overshoot (TTM purchases $147.3B vs OCF $148.5B) without AWS, ads, and silicon returns showing up in normalized free cash flow per share.
AWS growth deceleration and margin compression from hyperscaler price competition.
